test_description: This test case evaluates the RAG system's robustness in retrieving a specific, rare, or hard-to-find fact (the 'needle') from a large context ('haystack').
question: [Fill] The record-high hole mobility achieved in Ge (110)/<110> GAA nanosheet p-FETs, normalized by footprint to account CFET drivability, is _____ cm2/V·s.

gold_context_sources.section_title: Ge(110) GAA Nanosheet / Si(100) Tri-gate Nanosheet Monolithic CFETs Featuring Record-high Hole Mobility
gold_context_sources.evidence: Notably, we achieved a record-high hole mobility of 1200 cm2/V∙s (normalized by footprint to account CFET drivability) in the Ge (110)/<110> GAA nanosheet p-FETs with flat top and bottom surfaces.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is 1200. Reasoning: The exact phrase in the context states that a record-high hole mobility of 1200 cm2/V∙s normalized by footprint was achieved in Ge (110)/<110> GAA nanosheet p-FETs, which matches the fill-in-the-blank requirement precisely.
